# How to Build Digital Humans? From Priors to Photorealistic Avatars

> Companion website for the Eurographics 2026 State-of-the-Art Report on controllable 3D human avatar creation.

## Authors

- Wojciech Zielonka (Meta)
- Tobias Kirschstein (Technical University of Munich)
- Timo Bolkart (Google)
- Simon Giebenhain (Technical University of Munich)
- Vanessa Sklyarova (Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems / ETH Zurich)
- Xiang Deng (Tsinghua University)
- Donglai Xiang (NVIDIA)
- Shunsuke Saito (Meta)
- Yebin Liu (Tsinghua University)
- Matthias Niessner (Technical University of Munich)
- Justus Thies (Technical University of Darmstadt)

## Scope

This survey covers the field of digital human avatar creation, organized into three stages: (i) learning priors of human appearance and motion, (ii) creating a personalized avatar, and (iii) animating the avatar. The focus is on prior learning and avatar creation. It reviews methods for full-body and head avatars, as well as layered representations that decompose the body into components such as hands, hair, and garments.

## Structured Data

### Publications Database

- URL: assets/data/publications.json
- Format: JSON array of publication entries
- Description: Machine-readable database of all surveyed publications with metadata and taxonomy classification.

### BibTeX Bibliography

- URL: tables_src/bibliography.bib
- Format: BibTeX
- Description: Full bibliography of all surveyed papers. Citation key format is `SurnameOfFirstAuthor + Year + MethodAcronym` (e.g., `Lombardi21mvp`). Each entry includes metadata comments for webpage, code, video, and arxiv links.

## Taxonomy

### Table Types

Each publication is classified into one of two table types:

- `avatar`: Methods that create animatable or controllable digital human avatars.
- `assets`: Methods that create static 3D assets (geometry, appearance) without animation control.

### Body Part Categories (Contents field)

Publications are tagged with one or more body regions:

- `Full-body`: Whole-body avatar or asset
- `Face`: Face-only methods
- `Hair`: Hair modeling or reconstruction
- `Hands`: Hand-specific methods
- `Garment`: Clothing and garment modeling

Entries may combine multiple categories (e.g., `"Full-body, Garment"`, `"Face, Hair"`).

### Venues

Common publication venues include: CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, NeurIPS, ICLR, SIGGRAPH, SIGGRAPH Asia, TOG (SIGGRAPH), 3DV, arXiv, and others.

## Publication Entry Schema

Each entry in `publications.json` has the following structure:

```json
{
  "key": "string — unique citation key (e.g., 'wang2026flowact')",
  "title": "string — full paper title",
  "authors": "string — comma-separated author list in 'Last, First' format",
  "year": "string — publication year",
  "venue": "string — publication venue (e.g., 'CVPR', 'SIGGRAPH', 'arXiv')",
  "entry_type": "string — BibTeX entry type ('article', 'inproceedings', etc.)",
  "links": {
    "webpage": "string|null — project page URL",
    "code": "string|null — source code repository URL",
    "video": "string|null — demo or presentation video URL",
    "arxiv": "string|null — arXiv PDF URL"
  },
  "classification": {
    "table_type": "string — 'avatar' or 'assets'",
    "fields": {
      "...": "string — taxonomy fields vary by table_type (see below)"
    }
  },
  "note": "string|null — optional editorial note",
  "skip_reason": "string|null — reason if entry is excluded from display"
}
```

### Classification Fields for `avatar` table_type

- Prior Dataset Size
- Datasets
- Data Type (e.g., Real, Synthetic)
- Data Modality (e.g., Mono video, Multi-view video)
- Needed Assets (e.g., Registered Meshes, None)
- Input (e.g., Zero, One, Few)
- Additional Priors (e.g., Video Diffusion, FLAME, SMPL)
- Req. Optimization (Yes/No)
- Creation Speed (e.g., Instant, Minutes)
- Animation Signal (e.g., Audio, Text, Expression)
- Lighting Control (e.g., None, Relightable)
- Animation Speed (e.g., Real-time, Offline)
- Image Synthesis (e.g., Neural Rendering, Rasterization)
- Image Refinement (Yes/No)
- Contents (body region tags)

### Classification Fields for `assets` table_type

- Prior Dataset Size
- Datasets
- Data Type
- Data Modality
- Needed Assets
- Input
- Additional Priors
- Creation Speed
- Representation (e.g., 3DGS, NeRF, Mesh)
- Simulation Ready (Yes/No)
- Lighting Control
- Contents (body region tags)
